import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut start = -1
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(b)
      if b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 13 or b == 10
        if start >= 0
          let tok = string.substring(s, start, i)
          match string.parse_int(tok)
          when Some(v)
            result = list.push(result, v)
          when None
            ()
          end
          start = -1
        end
      else
        if start < 0
          start = i
        end
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if start >= 0
    let tok = string.substring(s, start, n)
    match string.parse_int(tok)
    when Some(v)
      result = list.push(result, v)
    when None
      ()
    end
  end
  result
end

fn find_pair(_ nums: List<Int>, _ target: Int) -> Unit
  let n = nums.len()
  mut i = 0
  mut found = false
  while i < n and not found
    mut j = i + 1
    while j < n and not found
      match nums.get(i)
      when Some(a)
        match nums.get(j)
        when Some(b)
          if a + b == target
            print("found=#{i},#{j}")
            found = true
          end
        when None
          ()
        end
      when None
        ()
      end
      j = j + 1
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if not found
    print("none")
  end
end

fn main() -> Unit
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(line1)
    let nums = parse_ints(line1)
    match io.read_line()
    when Some(line2)
      let t_tok = string.trim(line2)
      match string.parse_int(t_tok)
      when Some(target)
        find_pair(nums, target)
      when None
        print("none")
      end
    when None
      print("none")
    end
  when None
    print("none")
  end
end
